recommendations.title: 6 packages losing momentum
recommendations.detail: @ai-rpg-engine/starter-zombie, @mcptoolshop/escape-the-valley, @mcptoolshop/sovereignty and 3 more show sustained decline. Consider: release updates, fix open issues, or update documentation.

recommendations.title: High portfolio concentration
recommendations.detail: Gini coefficient 0.79 indicates downloads are heavily concentrated in a few packages. Diversify promotion efforts across the portfolio.

recommendations.title: Heavy npm dependency
recommendations.detail: 76% of downloads come from npm. Consider cross-publishing to PyPI and NuGet to reduce single-registry risk.

recommendations.title: 1 package with repeated spikes
recommendations.detail: Multiple download spikes detected for @ai-rpg-engine/asset-registry. Could indicate bot activity, viral posts, or dependency adoption.

actionableAdvice.title: 75 packages in critical health
actionableAdvice.detail: backpropagate, mcpt, headless-wheel-builder, voice-soundboard, file-compass scored below 20/100.
actionableAdvice.action: Review these packages for broken installs, outdated dependencies, or missing documentation. Consider archiving if no longer maintained.

actionableAdvice.title: Portfolio concentration risk
actionableAdvice.detail: Gini coefficient 0.79 — downloads are concentrated in a few packages.
actionableAdvice.action: Promote underperforming packages in README badges, blog posts, and release notes of popular packages.

actionableAdvice.title: 76% of traffic from npm
actionableAdvice.detail: Single-registry dependency increases blast radius if npm has outages or policy changes.
actionableAdvice.action: Cross-publish key packages to PyPI (via wrapper) or NuGet. Add install instructions for all registries in READMEs.

actionableAdvice.title: 3 packages with untapped potential
actionableAdvice.detail: @mcptoolshop/npm-launcher, @mcptoolshop/escape-the-valley, @mcptoolshop/sovereignty have active users but stalled growth.
actionableAdvice.action: Add new features, improve docs, or create tutorials. Small efforts can shift these from D to C+ quickly.
